title: brickellia coulteri var. coulteri
intro: Brickellia coulteri var. coulteri, also known as Coulter's brickellia, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, derived from its essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Coulter's brickellia include relief from symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps. The herbal preparations of Brickellia coulteri var. coulteri include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
